name: Homeostatic Reinforcement Learning (Drive-Reduction MDP)
description: A tabular Q-learning agent where reward is redefined as drive reduction — the decrease in a quadratic discomfort function measuring deviation from a homeostatic set point. The agent maintains an internal energy state, discretizes its combined state (energy bin + relative direction to nearest food) into a finite state space, and learns action values via temporal-difference (TD) updates. Action selection uses softmax (Boltzmann) exploration over Q-values. This formulation directly implements the homeostatic RL framework of Keramati & Gutkin (2011), where behavior emerges from minimizing a multi-variable drive function, mapping homeostatic regulation onto a standard MDP. Unlike the PI controller (Formulation 1), this agent has no hardcoded decision rules — all behavior is learned from the intrinsic drive-reduction reward signal.

variables.name: energy
variables.description: Internal energy level of the agent. Decreases each tick by passive decay, increases when food is consumed.

variables.name: drive
variables.description: Quadratic drive/discomfort function: D(x) = phi * (x - s)^2. Measures how far internal state is from set point.

variables.name: reward
variables.description: Drive reduction achieved by last action: r(t) = D(x(t)) - D(x(t+1)). Positive when drive decreases.

variables.name: q_table
variables.description: Tabular action-value function Q(z, a). Maps discretized state z and action a to expected cumulative discounted drive reduction.

variables.name: discretized_state
variables.description: Tuple (energy_bin, direction_to_nearest_food). energy_bin is floor(x * n_bins / x_max), direction is one of 9 values.

variables.name: previous_energy
variables.description: Energy level before the last action was taken, used to compute drive reduction reward.

variables.name: previous_state
variables.description: Previous discretized state, used for Q-table TD update.

variables.name: previous_action
variables.description: The action taken in the previous step, used for Q-table TD update.

parameters.name: drive_weight
parameters.source: Keramati & Gutkin (2011): per-variable weight, single resource here

parameters.name: energy_from_eating
parameters.source: Tuned to interaction with decay rate

parameters.name: td_learning_rate
parameters.source: Standard RL default; Sutton & Barto convention

rules.description: Energy dynamics: passive decay each tick, gain from eating
rules.pseudocode:
ate = 1 if (last_action == 'eat' and consumed) else 0
x = clamp(x - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, x_max)

rules.description: Drive function: quadratic discomfort from set point deviation
Reward as drive reduction: positive reward when drive decreases
rules.pseudocode:
D_prev = phi * (x_prev - s) ** 2
D_curr = phi * (x - s) ** 2
r = D_prev - D_curr

rules.description: State discretization: bin energy level and encode direction to nearest food
rules.pseudocode:
energy_bin = min(int(x * n_bins / x_max), n_bins - 1)
if no food visible:
    direction = 'none'
else:
    nearest_food = food with minimum Manhattan distance from (pos_x, pos_y)
    dx = nearest_food.x - pos_x
    dy = nearest_food.y - pos_y
    direction = compass_direction(dx, dy)  # one of N,NE,E,SE,S,SW,W,NW, or 'here'
z = (energy_bin, direction)
delta = r + gamma * max(Q[z, a_prime] for a_prime in actions) - Q[z_prev, a_prev]
Q[z_prev, a_prev] = Q[z_prev, a_prev] + alpha * delta

decision_logic.description: Q-learning agent with softmax exploration. On each step: (1) update energy state, (2) compute drive-reduction reward from previous action, (3) discretize current state, (4) perform TD update on Q-table for previous state-action, (5) select new action via softmax over Q-values. Early in learning the agent explores broadly; as Q-values converge, it reliably moves toward food when energy is below set point and stays when near set point.
pseudocode: # Store the action we just took
x = clamp(x - d + delta_eat * ate, 0, x_max)
food_at_position = any(f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1] for f in food_list)
nearest = min(food_list, key=lambda f: abs(f['x']-pos[0]) + abs(f['y']-pos[1]))
# Step 4: TD update (skip on first step)
best_future = max(Q.get((z, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
delta = r + gamma * best_future - Q.get((z_prev, a_prev), 0.0)
Q[(z_prev, a_prev)] = Q.get((z_prev, a_prev), 0.0) + alpha * delta
max_q = max(v for v in q_vals.values() if v != float('-inf'))
exp_vals = {a: (math.exp(beta * (v - max_q)) if v != float('-inf') else 0) for a, v in q_vals.items()}
probs = {a: ev / total for a, ev in exp_vals.items()}
# Step 6: Store state for next update

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food'] — list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.ate_food: last_action == 'eat' and perception['last_action_result'] indicates consumption success

env_mapping.reward_source: Internal drive-reduction reward r(t) = D(x(t)) - D(x(t+1)); environment reward from eat action (1.0) is NOT used for decision-making

expected_behaviors.description: Drive increases quadratically as energy falls below set point without eating
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 30 steps with no food → assert D increases each step and D = phi * (x - s)^2

expected_behaviors.description: Eating produces positive reward (drive reduction) when energy is below set point
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: at x=50, eat food (gaining delta_eat) → compute r = D(50) - D(65) → assert r > 0

expected_behaviors.description: Eating produces negative reward (drive increase) when energy would overshoot above set point
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: at x=78, eat food (gaining 15 → x=93) → D(78)=4, D(93)=169 → assert r < 0

expected_behaviors.description: Q-values converge to make agent move toward food when energy is low
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 500 steps with food available → assert Q[(low_energy_bin, 'E'), 'move_right'] > Q[(low_energy_bin, 'E'), 'move_left'] when food is to the east

expected_behaviors.description: Agent learns to stay when energy is near set point and no food is needed
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after training, set x ≈ s → assert most frequently selected action is 'stay'

expected_behaviors.description: Softmax exploration decreases as Q-values differentiate
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: compare action entropy at step 10 vs step 500 → assert entropy decreases

references: Keramati, M. & Gutkin, B. (2011) — A Reinforcement Learning Theory for Homeostatic Regulation — NeurIPS
Yin, H. (2025) — Linking Homeostasis to Reinforcement Learning: Internal State Control and Behavior — ScienceDirect
Cannon, W.B. (1932) — The Wisdom of the Body
Bernard, C. (1865) — Introduction à l'étude de la médecine expérimentale
Sutton, R.S. & Barto, A.G. (2018) — Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction (learning rate / discount conventions)
